description: A too-tight watermark silently discards valid late events, undercounting aggregates.
body: Failure mode: the watermark delay and allowed lateness are set tighter than the source's real out-of-order skew, so windows close before stragglers arrive and valid late events are dropped without error. Detection signals: a non-trivial dropped-late-record metric, and aggregates that come in lower than a batch reconciliation over the same period. Do not accept the lower numbers as correct. Safer fallback: measure the actual arrival-vs-event-time distribution, widen the watermark bound and allowed lateness to cover it, route remaining stragglers to a side output for accounting, and re-reconcile against the batch reference.
